summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on three zoning cases. They approved the HealthSource Addition Final Development Plan, approved Section 1 of Woodall Crossing Final Development Plan with conditions including privacy fences and garage setback modifications, and approved Section 3 of Cova-Barclay Woods Final Development Plan with conditions requiring Section 2 approval before permits and additional landscaping buffering. Discussion also covered upcoming zoning workshops and code review topics.
decisions: Approved video meeting minutes from November 12, 2024
Approved Final Development Plan for HealthSource Addition (ZC 2024-007)

Approved Final Development Plan for Woodall Crossing Section 1 (ZC 2023-007) with conditions including privacy fences, Board of Trustees stipulations from 12-12-23, and garage setback modification at staff discretion

Approved Final Development Plan for Cova-Barclay Woods Section 3 (ZC 2022-003) with conditions: no permits until Section 2 FDP approved, additional landscaping for 100% screening along lots 113-134, planting list per Section 10.04
action_items: Cova-Barclay developer to submit Section 2 Final Development Plan by early February for March Zoning Commission meeting
Cova-Barclay developer to update Section 3 plan with additional landscaping for near 100% screening in buffer area along lots 113-134
Alyssa (staff) to send deadline date for March meeting submissions to applicant
Christy Norman to relay HOA snow removal/maintenance concerns from Section 2 residents to developer
Staff to begin planning zoning workshops for late February/March timeframe
Board members to provide recommendations on zoning code topics for workshops
key_amounts: 54 buildable lots on 10 acres for Woodall Crossing Section 1
42 buildable lots for Cova-Barclay Woods Section 3
7,625 sq ft minimum lot area for Woodall Crossing Section 1
8,520 sq ft minimum lot area for Cova-Barclay Section 3
1,400 sq ft minimum floor area for Cova-Barclay homes
20-foot perimeter buffer area required for Cova-Barclay Section 3
85 lots allowed before ODOT traffic signal application required for Woodall Crossing
notable_topics: HealthSource Addition Final Development Plan approval including sidewalk additions
Woodall Crossing (formerly Reserves of Barclay Woods) Section 1 Final Development Plan with garage setback modification discussion
Cova-Barclay Woods Section 3 Final Development Plan and issue of Section 2 never receiving formal FDP approval despite homes being built
Traffic impact study and ODOT traffic signal application requirement for Woodall Crossing
Landscaping and buffering requirements for residential developments
HOA maintenance concerns raised regarding snow removal in Cova-Barclay Section 2
Upcoming zoning code workshops including accessory dwelling units and garage standards
notes: Cova-Barclay Section 2 had zoning permits issued and houses built without the Zoning Commission ever formally approving the Final Development Plan - the record plat was signed but the FDP 'fell through the cracks.' The transcript has significant portions of garbled/corrupted text likely from automated transcription errors. Meeting was a Zoning Commission meeting, not a Board of Trustees meeting despite repeated automated headers in transcript.
title: ZC 1-7-25
